description_long: This flower object features a delicate, irregularly shaped corolla with petals that appear thin, soft, and somewhat translucent. The petals are arranged in a loose, somewhat radial pattern, with some overlapping and curling at the edges, giving the flower a slightly ruffled appearance. The central part of the flower is more compact, with a mix of white, yellow, and hints of pink or red, suggesting the presence of reproductive structures or a color gradient. The outer petals are predominantly pale grayish-white, with subtle textural variations and a matte finish. There is no visible stem or leaves attached to the flower, and the overall form is somewhat flattened and asymmetrical. The flower appears lightweight and fragile, typical of a freshly fallen or plucked blossom.
description: A delicate, pale flower with loosely arranged, ruffled petals and a soft, matte texture. The center shows hints of white, yellow, and pink, while the outer petals are grayish-white.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full spread of the ruffled, pale petals and the compact, multicolored center.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin, soft petals and the slight curvature and layering around the center.
description_view_2: Bottom view with the flower appearing mostly dark, with only the underside of the central area and some petal edges visible.
description_view_3: Angled view emphasizing the irregular, wavy edges of the petals and the flower's overall asymmetry.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a loose, irregular radial pattern with overlapping and some curling at the edges, lacking strict symmetry and forming a ruffled corolla.
